---
title: "implicit_minimize"
generated: true
description: "Differentiable argmin of a scalar objective by the implicit theorem."
---

[‚Äπ back to `nitrix.linalg.optimize`](../nitrix-linalg-optimize.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:implicit_minimize -->
```python
implicit_minimize(objective_fn: ScalarObjectiveFn, data: Any, x0: Float[Array, ' p'], *, maxiter: int = 50, ridge: float = 0.0, cg_tol: float = 1e-06, cg_maxiter: Optional[int] = None) -> Float[Array, ' p']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:implicit_minimize -->
Differentiable argmin of a scalar objective by the implicit theorem.

Compute the argmin of a scalar objective $f(\mathrm{data}, x)$,
differentiable with respect to `data` by the **implicit-function
theorem**.

The general-objective counterpart of [`implicit_least_squares`](../nitrix-linalg-optimize/implicit_least_squares.qmd): where
that assumes a least-squares residual and uses the Gauss-Newton Hessian
$J^{\top}J$, this differentiates the argmin of an *arbitrary* scalar
$f$ through the **exact** Hessian $\nabla^2_x f$ -- so a
registration whose cost is LNCC / MI / correlation-ratio (not a
least-squares residual) becomes a differentiable layer too.

The forward solve is BFGS (`jax.scipy.optimize.minimize`); the
backward uses the stationarity
$g(\mathrm{data}, x^*) = \nabla_x f = 0$, which by the
implicit-function theorem gives
$\partial x^* / \partial \mathrm{data} = -(\nabla^2_x f)^{-1}
\partial_{\mathrm{data}} g$.  The adjoint
$(\nabla^2_x f)\, w = \bar{x}$ is solved matrix-free with
[`cg`](../nitrix-linalg-krylov/cg.qmd) (Hessian-vector products via `jax.linearize` of the gradient
-- stays on-device), then $-w$ is pushed back through
$\partial_{\mathrm{data}} g$.

**Memory footprint.**  $O(1)$ in the BFGS iteration count -- nothing
is unrolled.  The Hessian is never materialised: [`cg`](../nitrix-linalg-krylov/cg.qmd) applies it as a
matrix-free Hessian-vector product, so the backward holds only the
[`cg`](../nitrix-linalg-krylov/cg.qmd) iterates (a handful of `x`-shaped vectors, $O(P)$ in the
parameter count) plus the `jax.linearize` cache of a *single*
$\nabla_x f$ evaluation.  For an image-similarity objective the
latter dominates and scales as the cost of one gradient -- $O(M)$ in
the number of voxels -- *not* $O(P^2)$: the dense $P \times P$
Hessian is never formed.  So peak backward memory is $O(M + P)$,
independent of both the iteration count and $P^2$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:implicit_minimize -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `objective_fn` | `ScalarObjectiveFn` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | Map `(data, x) -> scalar` (`data` the differentiable argument, an array or pytree; `x` the optimisation variable, `(P,)`, mapping to a scalar objective). |
| `data` | `Any` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | The differentiable parameters / inputs (an array or pytree). |
| `x0` | `Float` | `p` | ‚Äî | Initial guess for `x`, `(P,)`. |
| `maxiter` | `int` | ‚Äî | `50` | Forward BFGS iteration cap. |
| `ridge` | `float` | ‚Äî | `0.0` | Non-negative Tikhonov ridge added to the Hessian in the backward adjoint solve (default `0`); raise it if the Hessian at the optimum is near-singular (a minimum's Hessian is only positive semidefinite). |
| `cg_tol` | `float` | ‚Äî | `1e-06` | Backward adjoint-solve ([`cg`](../nitrix-linalg-krylov/cg.qmd)) controls. |
| `cg_maxiter` | `Optional[int]` | ‚Äî | `None` | Backward adjoint-solve ([`cg`](../nitrix-linalg-krylov/cg.qmd)) controls. |

**Returns** `Float` ‚Äî shape `p`. The minimiser $x^*$, `(P,)`. $\mathrm{d}x^* / \mathrm{d}x_0 = 0$ (the initial guess does not affect the gradient).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:implicit_minimize -->
::: {.callout-note}
The implicit gradient is exact only at a true stationary point (the
backward assumes $\nabla_x f(\mathrm{data}, x^*) = 0$), so run the
forward solve to convergence.  At a minimum $\nabla^2_x f$ is
positive semidefinite, so the SPD [`cg`](../nitrix-linalg-krylov/cg.qmd) applies -- pass `ridge > 0`
if it is only semidefinite.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:implicit_minimize -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
